examples: It was as wide and straight as a city street, yet it seemed untraveled.
How softly the turf had carpeted the untraveled avenue — he could no longer feel the roadway beneath his feet!
Wherever possible he tries to take roads like those on the original route--narrow, twisting and today largely untraveled.
In any case, Thompson's prescription takes a road largely untraveled in a party that has often suffered politically when raising Social Security as an issue.
Most employees have been with the company for more than a decade, and the knowledgeable guides have the inside track on untraveled roads for game drives in the busy Serengeti.
